title: fix: set Cloudflare account for frontend deploy
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` to the `pollinations.ai` production deploy workflow.
- Fixes Wrangler failing in GitHub Actions when the token can access multiple Cloudflare accounts.
- Validated YAML parsing and `git diff --check`; Biome ignores workflow YAML in this repo.

gist.headline: Wrangling the Wrangler
gist.blurb: Fixed a tiny hiccup in our automated deployment pipeline. The frontend now knows exactly which Cloudflare account to call home.
gist.summary: Added the CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID to the frontend production deployment workflow to resolve Wrangler authentication failures in GitHub Actions.
gist.impact: This ensures reliable, automated deployments for the main pollinations.ai website by explicitly targeting the correct Cloudflare account.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee wearing a tiny hard hat, fixing a glowing orange/blue pipe (representing Cloudflare/CI pipeline) with a wrench, while the Monitor Robot holds a clipboard with a green checkmark.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny yellow hard hat, using a small wrench to tighten a glowing orange and blue pipe. A chunky pixel robot with a CRT monitor head stands nearby holding a clipboard with a green checkmark. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel blue gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, chunky sprites.
